summary:
- GitHub Copilot is integrated into VS Code but not installed by default; users must sign in and enable it, so it may not count in installation stats without explicit activation.
- The discussion suggests checking if Kilo Code should be added to the list of discussed tools.
- There is interest in whether GitHub Copilot installations include all VS Code instances or only those with the extension explicitly installed and activated.
- A suggestion was made to overlay a Hacker News sentiment chart for these tools.
- Clarification is needed on how GitHub Copilot's installation metrics are measured, particularly if the integrated version in VS Code counts without explicit extension installation.
